client.name: FreelanceFlow
client.description: A project management and invoicing platform built specifically for independent freelancers and small creative studios.
features: Project boards with Kanban and timeline views
Time tracking with automatic timesheet generation
Client portal for approvals and feedback
client.target_audience: Solo freelancers and micro-studios (1-5 people) in creative fields: designers, developers, writers, consultants

competitors.name: Asana
strengths: Strong enterprise adoption and brand recognition
weaknesses: Per-user pricing becomes expensive for small teams

competitors.name: Trello
strengths: Low learning curve, loved by non-technical users
weaknesses: Limited to Kanban — no timeline, Gantt, or workload views
No invoicing, contracts, or client-facing features

competitors.name: Bonsai
strengths: All-in-one for freelancers: contracts, proposals, invoicing, time tracking
weaknesses: Project management features are basic compared to Asana/Trello
Higher price point for full feature set
competitors.market_share: ~2% of project management SaaS market, leading in freelancer-specific niche

trends: AI-assisted project planning and scope estimation becoming table stakes
Freelancer workforce growing 25% YoY — 76M freelancers in US alone
Shift toward all-in-one platforms over best-of-breed point solutions
Increasing demand for client-facing portals and collaboration features
Usage-based and outcome-based pricing gaining traction over per-seat models

user_sentiment: FreelanceFlow users love the invoicing integration — 'Finally I don't need a separate app for billing'
Common complaint: 'I wish the project management was as good as Asana's — FreelanceFlow feels limited for complex projects'
Power users request: 'Please add Gantt charts and resource planning'
Positive: 'The AI scope estimation saved me from underquoting twice this month'
Negative: 'Only 5GB of file storage is not enough for designers sharing large PSD files'
Positive: 'Client portal is a game changer — clients stop emailing me for updates'
Negative: 'No mobile app yet, I manage everything from my phone'
